entry_points.description: Login endpoint accepting username and password as GET query parameters; directly interpolates them into a raw SQL query string with no sanitization or parameterization

mechanisms: credential check via raw SQL query
auth.notes: Passwords stored in plaintext in the in-memory SQLite database. Authentication performed by comparing raw plaintext username and password via a concatenated SQL string. No session, token, or cookie management. No logout mechanism.

data_flows.name: Login credential check
data_flows.validation: None — no input validation, sanitization, or type checking performed
data_flows.processing: Username and password are directly interpolated into a raw SQL template literal string: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '${username}' AND password = '${password}'
data_flows.storage: In-memory SQLite database (users table with id, username, password columns); seeded with hardcoded admin:password123 credential
data_flows.output: HTTP text response: 'Login successful!' if a row is returned, 'Login failed' otherwise, 'Error' on DB error
data_flows.sanitization_notes: No sanitization of any kind. Query is built via ES6 template literal string concatenation. No use of parameterized queries or prepared statements. Raw query is also logged to stdout via console.log.

trust_boundaries.description: HTTP client (browser or API caller) to Express application on port 3000
trust_boundaries.enforcement: No CORS policy, no helmet headers, no rate limiting, no input validation, no authentication middleware. All query parameters accepted as-is.

modules.name: application
modules.description: Entire application in a single file. Initializes Express server, creates in-memory SQLite DB with a users table, seeds one hardcoded admin user with plaintext password, and exposes a single GET /login route that constructs a raw SQL query from unsanitized query parameters.

security_observations: GET query parameters used to transmit credentials — username and password are visible in URL, server logs, browser history, and proxy logs
No parameterized queries or prepared statements: SQL query built via ES6 template literal string interpolation enabling classic SQL injection
Raw SQL query including user-supplied credentials is logged to stdout via console.log on every request
Passwords stored in plaintext in the database — no hashing algorithm applied
No input validation, length limits, or type checking on any user-supplied input
No authentication middleware or session management — no tokens, cookies, or session store
No rate limiting on the login endpoint — brute force attacks are unrestricted
No error differentiation — raw DB errors returned directly to the client
No security headers middleware (e.g., helmet) configured
Application listens on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) on port 3000 with no TLS/HTTPS
